summary: Merged the native C# Aquarium Engine line to main and made it the live development branch. Removed branch-lineage memorial docs so the repo describes the current engine instead of carrying prototype-era debris.

Added Aquarium-owned persistent state machinery: AGENTS.md for local operating doctrine, state/map.yaml for canonical project map, state/memory.json for durable renderer/state/UI lessons, state/evidence.jsonl for compact lesson entries, and state/scratch.md for disposable working context.

Distilled only engine-relevant doctrine from the older Aquarium state: camera/Grid invariants, world-space anchors/domains, shader-owned visible world, conservative raymarch broad phase, diegetic lighting, DirectWrite overlay text, CultCache reload state, and hot reload failure behavior.

Ported the Aetheria UI isoline idea into the Aquarium Grid shader: height contours and gradient-angle field lines now derive from the deferred Grid height field, and Cartesian Gridlines use fwidth-based masks so line width stays stable in pixels across zoom.

Fixed dev-reload so headless smoke runs use separate PID state and logs from the visible dev window, preventing headless verification from stopping the active window. Added -Reopen to relaunch the last visible slot without rebuilding.
verification: visible Aquarium PID remained running after headless smoke

summary: Replaced the shader fixed raymarch far distance with a per-frame value equal to camera distance from the Grid origin plus Grid radius, matching the circular Grid fade boundary.

dev-watch now fingerprints the AquariumFrame cbuffer in Aquarium.hlsl as host contract input. Ordinary shader edits can still hot reload, but cbuffer layout changes trigger a host reload so C# frame constants and shader reads stay in sync.

Preserved a realtime volumetrics research map covering Bruneton atmospheric scattering, Wronski AC4 volumetric fog, Hillaire/Frostbite froxel volumetrics and sky/clouds, Dreams Learning from Failure, GigaVoxels, Horizon clouds, Gobo stable volume sampling, NVIDIA GDC volumetric scattering, and Unreal volumetric fog docs. Distilled the Aquarium path: explicit renderer-owned low-resolution volume pass first, Bruneton LUT atmosphere later, sparse brick streaming only if scale demands it.
verification: downloaded local source cache under research/volumetrics/sources
source cache and extracts gitignored due binary size and copyright

lesson: Follow-up scan through newer Advances/citation trails keeps Wronski/Hillaire froxel fog as Aquarium pass one. Nubis 2022/2023 is later cloud-asset work; AMD/Brixelizer-style GPU SDF points to cascaded sparse bricks with static/dynamic and toroidal partial updates; Neural Light Grid says learned fields are preprocessing/storage tools until runtime decode is cheap and inspectable.

Do not treat slide decks or presenter notes as transcripts. yt-dlp subtitle pulls for Dreams and GPUOpen sparse SDF materially changed the distilled lesson: Dreams preserved the evaluator/field into a painterly splat renderer after SDF, brick, hybrid transparency, and froxel-lighting attempts failed; Brixelizer is primarily an update/integration system with caps and debug tracing, not just an SDF sampler.

Sea of Thieves clouds provide a stylized counterexample to dense cloud raymarching: Rare used authored opaque polygonal cloud geometry, low-res blurred color/depth compositing, approximate world-position reconstruction, noise/distortion, synchronized wrapped wind offsets, and pressure zones so clouds could be readable world/event objects like skulls, ships, and storms. For Aquarium, symbolic weather/field omens may want geometry/field silhouettes plus cloudy compositing before volumetric traversal.

Documented the SDF field renderer plan and started a local SDF cloud prototype. Aquarium/Aetheria clouds are local world media that may be above, below, around, or enclosing the camera, so the core cloud architecture must not rely on skybox, hemisphere, paraboloid, or far-only assumptions. First implementation integrates four bounded analytic SDF cloud media along the camera ray before surface composition.

The first SDF cloud transfer function made nearer ellipsoid edges occlude farther clouds because the SDF boundary produced a positive-distance absorbing veil and too much extinction. Cloud SDFs should provide sculptural containment and empty-space skipping, while density should be eroded interior occupancy with feathered boundaries and low extinction until a real volume pass owns the transfer function.

Fainter extinction did not fix near-cloud silhouettes because the prototype was still structurally wrong: a global SDF shell march could spend the frame's cloud sample budget inside a nearer ellipsoid and fail to reach farther cloud intervals. The analytic prototype now intersects ray/ellipsoid intervals, jumps empty space to true entries, and samples density only inside container volumes.

The SDF field/cloud renderer should use a waterfall implementation shape, not a chain of shy compromised prototypes. Define the target machine first: field ABI, broad phase, explicit local volume pass, composition contract, and acceleration gates. The current fullscreen analytic cloud code is temporary scaffolding for inspection only, not a place to accumulate features.

Removed the analytic SDF cloud scaffold from visible scene composition and made the Grid a transparent schematic overlay. Bodies now trace as solid scene content; the Grid traces separately and blends lines/isolines/field lines/weather tint over the result. This preserves legibility while reserving real scene mass for the future explicit volumetric field renderer.

Diegetic Grid transparency over raymarched content should use Aetheria-style stochastic coverage, not alpha blending. Aquarium now ports the dither-clip path from Aetheria's Dither Functions and Dithered Particles shaders using the copied LDR_LLL1_0 blue-noise texture, and it only draws the Grid when the Grid hit is closer than the nearest solid body so planets are not overlaid.

dev-watch must treat runtime content as restart-owned input. Shader hot reload can swap HLSL, and live reload can swap Aquarium.Engine.Live, but new files under src/Aquarium.Engine/Assets require rebuilding the apphost slot and restarting the process so the renderer loads them.
verification: asset fingerprint check includes Aetheria-LDR_LLL1_0.png and .r8

lesson: Added a clean-room TSR-inspired Gate 1 temporal resolver instead of Playdead-style TAA. Aquarium now renders linear HDR scene color plus ray travel, applies Halton jitter, reprojects current world hits into the previous camera basis, clamps history to the current 3x3 neighborhood, rejects by travel delta, writes ping-pong history, and tonemaps after resolve.

TAA Gate 2A adds renderer metadata to history validation. Scene and history now carry material id plus surface normal; resolve rejects material mismatches and normal discontinuities. Grid stochastic surfaces write Grid travel/normal even on dither-miss pixels, preventing temporal accumulation from reprojecting against the body or background behind the diegetic Grid surface.

TAA Gate 2B replaces coarse material identity with stable field ids and adds analytic object-motion reprojection for orbiting SDF bodies. Planet hits now move from current planet center to previous planet center before history projection, so history follows rigid body motion instead of relying only on camera reprojection.

TAA Gate 3A adds a current-frame temporal-control target separate from field/normal metadata. It carries reactive strength, stochastic/composition coverage, a reserved medium-opacity slot, and a spare channel; the resolve now reduces history for low-coverage reactive Grid samples while keeping the volumetric contract explicit for later medium history.

TAA Gate 3B gives temporal-control data its own history ping-pong. Resolve now samples previous control at the reprojected UV and reduces history across stochastic coverage or reserved medium-opacity discontinuities, giving future volumetrics a real continuity signal instead of a one-frame mask.

TAA Gate 3C stores accepted history age in temporal-control history w. The resolver grows age only when validation passes, resets it on failure, and ramps history authority with age so a newly reprojected sample can contribute without immediately carrying full stable-history weight.

TAA Gate 3D fixes travel validation under camera motion. Previous history travel must be compared to the reprojected previous-world position's distance from the previous camera, not to current-frame travel; otherwise stable history is falsely rejected whenever camera motion changes ray distance.

TAA Gate 3E fixes stochastic Grid accumulation. Opaque-surface neighborhood clamping and current-frame color-delta rejection collapse Grid history on dither-miss frames because the current sample looks like background; Grid history now bypasses those color checks and validates through field, travel, normal, coverage, reactive, and age terms.

Temporal debugging must be visible in the running window, not hidden behind relaunch-only command flags. F1 cycles render debug modes and 0-4 select final, raw current scene, reprojected history, history age, and history weight, making it possible to prove whether the presented frame is resolved and whether history is alive.

dev-watch must not only classify source changes; it must also verify the recorded script-owned process is alive. If the visible/headless PID recorded by dev-reload is gone or no longer belongs to the recorded slot, the watcher relaunches from a fresh slot. The watch fingerprint also includes scripts/*.ps1 so script changes match the documented restart-owned contract.

TAA Gate 3F gives stochastic Grid coverage a high-retention temporal blend. Coverage and reactive strength must not reduce Grid history authority because low coverage is exactly where temporal accumulation is required; Grid now validates with travel, field, normal, coverage continuity, medium continuity, and age while using a higher max history weight than opaque surfaces.

TAA Gate 3G fixes foreground glow leaking into background Grid history. Identity/control buffers are now point-loaded and field ids use exact equality, while Grid history color uses nearest identity-aligned sampling instead of bilinear color sampling. Grid max history weight was also reduced from 0.975 to 0.94 to keep linework less over-smoothed.

TAA Gate 3H fixes Grid history smoke by separating temporal support from broad overlay alpha. Grid control coverage now stores line/contour/field-line support, not field tint alpha; current support gates Grid history, broad tint no longer keeps age alive across the surface, and Grid retention is reduced to 0.90 for crisper response.

TAA Gate 3I stops using raw stochastic Grid hit/miss color as the current radiance truth. For Grid pixels the resolve reconstructs the analytic overlay at the current world hit and uses premultiplied overlay color as the current estimate; history then stabilizes the estimate instead of trying to recover alpha from binary noise alone.

TAA Gate 3J restores debug mode 1 to true raw scene color and stops final Grid pixels from blending previous color history. The current Grid renderer now uses analytic current Grid color for final output and writes that estimate into history; history age/weight/color remain diagnostics until a real transparent/volumetric composition contract needs color history again.

TAA Gate 3L disables projection jitter in the live frame by scaling Halton jitter to zero. Visible judder on Self/planet surfaces means the resolver is not yet hiding sample offsets; jitter should stay disabled until opaque and Grid paths both prove stable.

Removed the abandoned unjittered Grid retrace after projection jitter was disabled. The retrace was a temporary splint that solved the Grid surface twice; with live jitter at zero, Grid analytic final color can reconstruct from the existing current hit without adding renderer Jenga.

Live runtime reload must be end-to-end acknowledged. dev-watch now treats live-current.txt as a request, not success: after building a live slot and updating the pointer, it waits for the running host stdout to report the exact live DLL path or marks the reload failed. Visible dev windows can use dev-watch -ReopenWhenClosed to reopen the last good slot without rebuilding after the window is closed.

dev-watch was not reaching its poll loop because Start-Process -Wait around dev-reload could remain attached to the long-lived launched Aquarium process after initial launch. The watcher now invokes dev-reload synchronously, leaving app process ownership inside dev-reload, and a live-only Aquarium.Engine.Live cosmetic change produced a live slot plus host acknowledgement without rebuilding the host.
verification: watch.log: Live reload requested, pointer updated, and acknowledged for artifacts/dev-reload/live-slots/20260509-000855-c3f6310c/Aquarium.Engine.Live.dll
latest.out.log: Live reload probe armed and Live runtime reload applied

lesson: Temporal debug modes now expose the resolver's control data directly. Modes 5 and 6 show current temporal control channels and current field identity, so Grid support, reactive strength, medium opacity, and field ownership can be inspected without inferring them from final color or history artifacts.
verification: Shader hot reload applied after Grid reactive channel fix
Mode 5 screenshot showed stale full-Grid reactive red; Grid reactive now zero and support remains coverage

lesson: Temporal control coverage now distinguishes empty background from stable surfaces. Background pixels default coverage to zero, opaque body hits write coverage 1, and Grid hits write line/support coverage; mode 5 should therefore show black empty background rather than green covered history everywhere.
verification: Shader hot reload applied with no stderr after control coverage fix

lesson: Gate 3M cuts temporal color history out of final presentation while projection jitter is disabled. The resolver still writes history, metadata, control, age, and weight for debug/future volumetrics, but mode 0 now presents current-frame color after analytic Grid reconstruction instead of letting stale opaque-surface history ghost the frame.
verification: Shader hot reload applied with no stderr after Gate 3M cut

lesson: Modern HDR/PBR presentation should be built as scene-linear lighting, explicit exposure, pre-tonemap bloom/veil, and an explicit display transform. Aquarium should avoid thresholded 2000s bloom; broad low-intensity bloom should spread existing HDR energy and be inspected with luminance/bloom debug views before SDF volumetrics land.
verification: research pass over ACES, Unreal exposure/bloom docs, Jimenez COD AW post-processing, Hable filmic tonemapping, and Frostbite PBR notes

lesson: Aquarium now has the first HDR presentation pipe before volumetrics: fixed manual exposure, half/quarter/eighth pre-tonemap bloom targets, firefly-safe downsampling, separable horizontal/vertical blur per level, low-gain bloom/veil composite before the ACES fit, and debug modes for bloom contribution and exposed luminance. This replaces the tempting full-res gather prototype with a proper hierarchical shape.

Aquarium debug UI now uses a native Direct2D/DirectWrite retained panel inspired by CultLib code-first composition: controls are bound by explicit read/write delegates and styled as flat compact rows rather than importing Unity prefabs or ImGui chrome. The first panel toggles with F2 and drives render debug mode plus HDR exposure, bloom intensity, and bloom veil through shader constants.

A startup EndOfStreamException from CultCache means the backing MessagePack snapshot itself is truncated, not that schema guardrails failed. Aquarium now quarantines unreadable snapshots beside the live cache and boots fresh typed state; CultLib single-file stores now write via same-directory temp file plus atomic replace so process death cannot leave a zero-byte live cache.
verification: zero-byte artifacts/dev-reload/cultcache/aquarium-client.msgpack quarantined and fresh 1773-byte cache created

lesson: Direct2D/Vortice Rect construction takes x, y, width, height. Aquarium UI code should use explicit RectFromEdges helpers whenever layout is expressed as left/top/right/bottom, otherwise sliders and fills can explode into full-frame slabs.

Debug UI interaction feedback should be element-specific and stable-geometry: base/hover/active states, slider track/fill and handle brighten independently, active brightens further, and saturated accent colors should lerp toward white rather than resizing controls or darkening surfaces.

Tooltips now belong to code-first control declarations as optional intent strings and render as viewport-clamped Direct2D overlay slabs. Keep that path for future renderer controls instead of adding separate anonymous tooltip plumbing.

Global renderer presentation knobs should be a typed CultCache document, not process-local debug UI state. Aquarium now uses a contracts-level GraphicsSettings DTO, a live epiphany.aquarium.graphics_settings document, host sync from renderer to runtime, and runtime flush before live assembly reload so render debug mode plus HDR exposure/bloom controls persist across restart and reload without forcing disk writes on every slider twitch.
verification: direct headless two-run probe: --render-debug 7 persisted as debug 7 on the next run using artifacts/verification/graphics-settings-persist.msgpack

lesson: The first fog-land implementation should establish the field contract and diagnostics before compositing fog. Aquarium now sends a fixed field instance structured buffer for Self, planets, and local medium ellipsoids to HLSL; modes 9 and 10 expose registered medium density and transmittance. Final mode 0 remains unchanged until the explicit low-res volume pass and compositor are built.
verification: direct headless startup with --render-debug 10 compiled shaders and loaded persisted debug mode 10

lesson: Registered medium diagnostics now come from a separate half-resolution medium pass instead of being integrated inside the scene shader. The pass samples the field instance buffer, writes density/transmittance/source into a medium texture, and scene/resolve stages sample that texture for debug/control. Final composition remains unchanged until the compositor is explicit.
verification: direct headless startup with --render-debug 11 compiled shaders and loaded persisted debug mode 11

lesson: Medium composition is now explicit and gated: the half-resolution medium pass writes diagnostic and transport textures, and resolve can composite surface * transmittance + in-scattering through a persisted Medium Composite setting. The default remains zero so final presentation does not change until the medium is intentionally enabled and tuned.
verification: direct headless startup in final mode with default Medium Composite 0

lesson: The first medium density view was too screen-ray dependent: it stored a completed half-res ray integral, so debug texture detail read as view-space soup. The pass now stores a 4x4 frustum slice atlas where each texel reconstructs a world point and evaluates field-local density; resolve integrates those stored cells front-to-back. Erosion noise is stationary in field-local space.

Do not label a view-dependent transport projection as Medium Density. Mode 9 now evaluates the registered medium field on a world-anchored Grid-altitude slice, while modes 10 and 11 remain frustum transport diagnostics. Density debugging and transport debugging are different tools.

A single Grid-altitude density slice can be legitimately blank when local media live above or below the Grid. Mode 9 is now a world-anchored density column over the Grid plane, sampling a fixed z range and visualizing max/mean density. Source debug is boosted independently from physical transport so near-black source patches do not masquerade as useful diagnostics.

Headless verification must wait for the Aquarium process to exit after its requested frames, not return when the process merely starts. Hidden headless windows prevent stuck smoke tests from leaving visible non-responsive windows, stdout startup checkpoints reveal where startup wedged, and shader debug loops that call the registered medium stack should stay rolled instead of forcing D3DCompiler to unroll a huge expansion.
verification: Get-Process Aquarium.Engine returned no remaining processes

lesson: Do not expose a Medium Source debug mode until the renderer has a real light-injection/scattering stage. Source means in-scattered radiance, not a tinted-density placeholder. Medium debugging should first show direct camera-ray density, direct camera-ray transmittance, and atlas/pass density so missing media can be diagnosed without semantic fog.

Ray-based debug views need a visible step selector instead of more opaque modes. DebugUi controls now support conditional visibility; the persisted MediumDebugStep slider appears only for medium ray preview modes and drives shader inspection of the selected raymarch sample or cumulative transmittance to that sample.

If the medium atlas density view shows fields but ray density/transmittance are empty at every step, check pass bindings before tuning density. The resolve shader also calls registeredMediumDensity for ray preview modes, so it must bind fieldInstances at t12 just like the medium and scene passes.

Do not compose the final frame from the diagnostic 4x4 medium atlas. If atlas density shows frustum-slice blobs while direct ray density is continuous, final composition should use direct registered-medium ray integration until a real froxel volume replaces the diagnostic atlas. Keep atlas density as an artifact-revealing debug view only.

The medium atlas trial has been replaced by a first real packed froxel volume: independent froxel dimensions, 32 depth slices, 8x4 D3D11 atlas packing, per-froxel density/transmittance/in-scattering, and resolve-side front-to-back composition. Direct ray density/transmittance debug remains the correctness reference; froxel density is the volume inspection view.

The Grid is a transparent schematic overlay, not a participating-medium occluder. Keep Grid travel/field id for temporal stability, but do not clamp fog integration to Grid travel; only solid fields such as Self and planets terminate the froxel composite ray.
verification: Full solution/headless verification blocked by dirty external CultLib changes on E:\Projects\CultLib codex/cultcache-attribute-first: CultCache.cs currently fails GameCult.Caching build with System.Text.Json/IsExternalInit errors

lesson: Before building the transparent stochastic surface pipe, keep D3D11 as the reference backend and add D3D12 beside it behind IAquariumRenderer. The first D3D12 pass should prove device, command queue, swapchain, RTV heap, command list, fence, clear, and present lifecycle with headless execution before porting scene passes.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 with latest known-good live assembly exited 0
Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d11 with latest known-good live assembly exited 0

lesson: Backend-neutral startup logging matters once D3D12 exists beside D3D11. Live runtime messages should describe Aquarium host invariants, not claim a specific graphics API that may be false for the selected renderer.
verification: Latest slot Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 and logged D3D12 device/swapchain creation

lesson: A useful D3D12 foothold must exercise shader and PSO machinery, not just ClearRenderTargetView. The D3D12 backend now uses per-swapchain-buffer command allocators, a pass context, fullscreen root signature, compiled smoke shader, PSO, viewport/scissor/RTV binding, and DrawInstanced fullscreen triangle submission.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 and logged fullscreen smoke pipeline creation

lesson: D3D12 descriptor/upload ownership is now represented in the backend instead of hiding inside a draw call. The smoke pass uses a shader-visible CBV/SRV/UAV descriptor arena, one upload constant buffer per swapchain frame, a CBV descriptor table at b0, and per-frame writes before command recording. Generalize this foothold into renderer-wide descriptor and upload ring helpers before porting Aquarium scene resources.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 with CBV descriptor table smoke pass

lesson: D3D12 now has reusable renderer-side primitives instead of smoke-pass-local plumbing: a descriptor arena, persistent mapped upload buffer, and default-heap render target with explicit state tracking. The smoke pass renders into the offscreen target and copies to the swapchain, proving render target lifetime and copy barriers before porting Aquarium scene/history/froxel textures.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 with offscreen render target copy path

lesson: D3D12 best-practice pass distilled Microsoft, NVIDIA, and AMD guidance. Current Aquarium D3D12 backend is acceptable bring-up scaffolding, but real pass migration should wait for a named resource registry, static/transient descriptor allocation, fence-reclaimed upload ring, debug names, PIX markers, and backbuffer state tracking. Committed resources and fixed arenas must not become production policy by inertia.
verification: Research sources reviewed via Microsoft Learn, DirectX Developer Blog, NVIDIA Technical Blog, and AMD GPUOpen
Current D3D12 helper code audited against descriptor, upload, barrier, memory, and fence guidance

lesson: Full-ass D3D12 foundation pass added debug names, command-list events, tracked swapchain backbuffer states, and per-frame persistent-mapped upload rings. This moves current code closer to the researched doctrine: visible ownership, inspectable GPU captures, and fence-safe transient CPU-to-GPU data. Next remaining pre-pass work is static/transient descriptor separation, resource registry, resize-safe recreation, and capacity diagnostics.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 with tracked resources, events, and upload ring

lesson: D3D12 now separates static shader descriptors from per-frame transient descriptor arenas, resets transient descriptors only after frame fence wait, registers named resources, and reports capacity for upload ring, transient/static shader descriptors, and RTV descriptors. Keep arenas simple until real churn demands free lists; avoid exact-fit capacities that leave no room for immediate next resources.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 and printed resource registry plus capacity diagnostics

lesson: D3D12 render targets now support SRV descriptors, and the smoke target is sampled by a second fullscreen pass instead of copied as an opaque resource. The renderer interface now receives current window dimensions, allowing D3D12 to recreate swapchain buffers and dependent smoke resources on resize. Resize still consumes fixed bring-up descriptors and fails loudly before exhaustion; production descriptor reclamation/rebuild policy is still needed before serious pass migration.
verification: Aquarium.Engine.exe --headless --renderer d3d12 exited 0 with SRV copy/display pass and capacity diagnostics

lesson: D3D12 render targets now support UAV descriptors, and the smoke pass proves a transient per-frame UAV table by writing a diagnostic texture from the pixel shader. Pixel shader UAV registers share the render-target output namespace, so the first manual UAV must bind at u1 when SV_Target0 is present. Resize now waits for the GPU, releases swapchain-dependent resources, rebuilds static shader/RTV descriptor arenas, and recreates descriptors from a clean state instead of burning fixed slots. The D3D12 backend also ignores the D3D11 shader-source override and compiles its copied D3D12Smoke.hlsl.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 20 with AQUARIUM_RENDERER=d3d12 exited 0 and printed UAV/static/transient capacity diagnostics

lesson: D3D12 resource capability must remain separate from descriptor ownership. A texture can be created with AllowUnorderedAccess for transient UAV views without allocating a persistent static UAV descriptor. The diagnostic UAV target now uses a transient per-frame UAV descriptor only, reducing static heap use and keeping descriptor ownership honest before real pass migration.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 20 with AQUARIUM_RENDERER=d3d12 exited 0 and static shader descriptors dropped to 1/64

lesson: The first real D3D12 pass migration exposed the Grid height target and frame-constant path, but its original fullscreen per-texel body loop was intentionally superseded by a brush-rendered pass. Keep temporal previous camera/Grid/time as renderer-level state; swapchain frame resources are allocator reuse buckets, not frame-history state.

The D3D12 Grid height pass must be a real gravity brush pass, not a fullscreen shader loop over every body per texel. It renders a base field, uploads a small body brush constant table, and draws one additive up-facing quad per body into a 128x128 scalar R16_Float target. R32G32B32A32_Float is the wrong target format for additive blending here, and extra channels are waste unless the pass contract actually stores extra data. The root signature must expose frame constants to the vertex shader because the brush vertex shader performs the top-down Grid-space orthographic transform.

A Grid height target is scalar. The D3D12 brush-rendered Grid height pass should use R16_Float and scalar pixel-shader outputs; four float channels were defensive waste after the R32G32B32A32_Float blend failure, not a real contract. Add channels only when the pass explicitly stores more than height.

D3D12 now has the first real Aquarium field data upload path: froxel primitive ids and field instances are built on CPU, uploaded through the per-frame upload ring, copied into default-heap structured buffers, and bound through transient SRV descriptors for a shader that actually reads them. Static and transient CBV/SRV/UAV heaps cannot be co-bound; pass-visible descriptor tables need to live in the active transient heap or be copied there. D3D11 is reference-only during migration, so duplicate table-building code is acceptable when it keeps the D3D12 path moving toward ownership.

D3D12 now renders the first medium froxel atlas pass into diagnostic and transport render targets from the field instance structured buffer. Render debug mode 11 displays D3D12 froxel density, and the old D3D12 Grid-height debug PSO was removed instead of preserving stale bring-up scaffolding.

D3D12 final mode now renders a first real scene pass instead of smoke: Self, planets, and medium transport are composed from D3D12 resources. Grid line transparency is injected into the medium froxel atlas as a thin participating layer and is not used as a scene-depth terminator; transparent surfaces should enter the same binned froxel/field integration path as volumetrics rather than alpha-blending as a separate class.

Transparent Grid contribution is now routed through an explicit transparent-surface table and froxel-binned transparent surface id buffer. The medium pass resolves transparent surface density/scattering by looking up the current froxel, so Grid/particles/billboards can share the same integration class instead of becoming alpha-blended scene-depth exceptions. The Medium Composite knob gates registered cloud media, while binned transparent surfaces still contribute to transport.

D3D12 presentation now mirrors the HDR doctrine: render scene-linear into an R16G16B16A16_Float scene target, build a three-level half/quarter/eighth bloom pyramid with firefly-safe downsample and separable blur scratch targets, then apply exposure, bloom/veil composite, and ACES only in the present pass. Debug mode 7 shows bloom contribution, mode 8 exposed luminance, and mode 11 remains a direct medium-density bypass.

D3D12 Grid transparency now carries the richer Aetheria/Aquarium line vocabulary inside the transparent-surface froxel path: cartesian gridlines, height isolines, and gradient-angle field lines are evaluated from the Grid height target and integrated as binned transparent medium contribution, not as a revived alpha-blended surface path.

D3D12 now has the first temporal diagnostic spine: the scene pass writes color/travel, field-id/normal metadata, and temporal-control MRTs, and the present shader can inspect current control in debug mode 5 and field identity in debug mode 6. This is not TAA parity yet; history ping-pong, reprojection, validation, and age/weight outputs still need to be ported before temporal accumulation is real.

D3D12 resolve now owns presentation and history output: it writes the swapchain plus ping-pong history color, metadata, and control targets. Opaque current scene hits reproject through previous camera/Grid state, remap orbiting planet hits to previous centers, and validate by field id, travel, normal, neighborhood color, coverage, and medium opacity before blending. Debug mode 3 shows history age and mode 4 history weight. Grid/transparent-specific temporal reconstruction remains pending.

Medium-only D3D12 pixels now get a volumetric temporal identity instead of being excluded from history: the scene pass stores FIELD_ID_MEDIUM, coverage from medium opacity/density, and travel from a density-weighted ray centroid. Resolve treats medium history as volumetric by skipping normal rejection, relaxing color rejection, and validating continuity through coverage and medium opacity. This stabilizes binned Grid/fog contribution without turning transparent surfaces back into alpha-depth surfaces.

D3D12 render debug modes 9 and 10 now match the D3D11 diagnostic contract: the resolve shader binds the field instance buffer and directly samples registered medium density/transmittance at the requested MediumDebugStep. These views are correctness probes independent of the froxel atlas; mode 11 remains the atlas density view.

D3D12 transparent stochastic surfaces now have a generic temporal event summary instead of piggybacking only on bulk medium identity. The medium pass writes a third froxel atlas target carrying transparent event support/density/radiance; the scene pass derives support-weighted travel and emits FIELD_ID_TRANSPARENT_EVENT; the resolve treats that identity as distributed medium-like history. Grid remains just the first producer of this path, not a special transparent surface exception.

D3D12 debug overlay parity uses the official D3D11On12 bridge to preserve native DirectWrite/Direct2D hinted text. The D3D12 frame graph owns scene rendering and leaves the backbuffer as a render target; the overlay briefly acquires the current wrapped swapchain image, draws the shared retained DebugUi, releases it to Present, and marks the tracked resource state. Keep this bridge narrow and overlay-only; diegetic text remains MSDF/SDF renderer work.

D3D12 is now the default Aquarium renderer. D3D11 remains selectable as the temporary reference backend, but host startup uses D3D12 unless --renderer or AQUARIUM_RENDERER overrides it. The D3D12 backend reports once-per-second steady-state CPU timing averages for total frame work, command-list recording, and the DirectWrite overlay bridge after skipping warmup frames so the bridge cost has a useful receipt while GPU timestamp instrumentation is still pending.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 30 without AQUARIUM_RENDERER exited 0 and logged D3D12 device/swapchain creation

lesson: D3D12 scene composition now uses one bounded ray interval traversal for solids, medium transport, and transparent events. Each interval samples medium/event transport, checks conservative start/mid/end froxel primitive bins for solid candidates, integrates partial transport up to the first opaque hit, and emits one ordered temporal packet. Current solid evaluators are analytic spheres, but future SDF surfaces should replace that evaluator inside this traversal rather than reintroducing a separate solid pass.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 30 with default D3D12 final mode exited 0
.\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 30 with default D3D12 field-identity mode exited 0

lesson: Grid linework must not be represented as a pre-averaged medium froxel summary. D3D12 now treats the Grid as a froxel-binned transparent candidate: the scene traversal intersects the Grid height sheet, samples the height texture at the ray hit, evaluates cartesian lines, height isolines, and gradient-angle field lines there, composites the event without terminating, and keeps marching. Future particles and billboards need the same candidate/event pipe with quad intersections and richer per-froxel event lists, not alpha side channels or atlas summaries.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 30 with default D3D12 final mode exited 0 and shader stderr empty

lesson: A transparent candidate bin is not useful if it marks every depth froxel. Grazing Grid views turned into soup because shallow rays could rediscover and composite the height sheet across multiple intervals. The Grid bin now covers only a conservative rendered-height slab, and the shader brackets the height-sheet crossing inside the active ray interval before solving it. Future transparent surface evaluators should use interval-local intersection/bracketing, not global root fishing from every candidate froxel.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 90 with default D3D12 final mode exited 0 and shader stderr empty

lesson: D3D12 shader/PSO creation must not block the startup/main-thread path. The renderer now starts shader pipeline builds on a background task, presents a lightweight loading clear until the first pipeline set is ready, polls editable D3D12 shader files for hot reload, swaps completed pipeline sets on the render thread after a GPU wait, and preserves the previous set on failure. Headless verification waits for a ready rendered frame and uses a separate headless CultCache to avoid colliding with the visible dev window.
verification: .\scripts\dev-reload.ps1 -Headless -RetainSlots 4 -HeadlessTimeoutSeconds 90 exited 0 after logging D3D12 shader pipelines ready

headless long-run with AQUARIUM_HEADLESS_READY_FRAMES=3000 touched D3D12Post.hlsl timestamp, logged D3D12 shader pipeline build started (hot reload), then logged D3D12 shader hot reload applied with empty stderr

lesson: Fixed view-travel slices are not a froxel traversal. Transparent Grid/particle surfaces need candidate discovery by spatial froxel DDA with exact cell intervals, event selection/deduplication, and shading/filtering after the canonical event is chosen so ddx/ddy are not evaluated inside divergent traversal. The headless smoke timeout must track the ready-frame contract; debug D3D12 PSO compilation can exceed 15 seconds while the app intentionally presents the loading frame.

Transparent surfaces must be binned into the view-froxel tube marched by the scene ray, not into the coarse 8x8x4 world broadphase. Aquarium now bootstraps this with a CPU-filled candidate buffer indexed by low-res screen cell and depth slice; this proves the topology, but the lasting implementation should be a GPU/raster binning pass to avoid large per-frame uploads.

View-froxel transparent lookup must not depend on the coarse world broadphase. A ray that misses the legacy 8x8x4 solid broadphase can still have valid transparent/medium candidates in its screen-space tube; only solid lookup should be gated by the world broadphase hit.

The D3D12 scene path must not keep a parallel world-space candidate broadphase. Solid primitives and transparent surfaces now both use view-froxel candidate buffers indexed by low-res screen cell and depth slice; the scene ray marches its assigned tube and tests candidates only inside each slice interval. CPU binning is temporary scaffolding until GPU/raster binning owns the producer side.

Representative-ray CPU binning is not conservative enough for view-froxel candidates; sampling only corners/center missed sub-cell silhouettes and chopped solids/Grid. Until GPU/raster binning owns the producer side, bootstrap candidate buffers should overfill and let exact per-slice intersection reject false positives.

View-froxel CPU bootstrap binning now follows the intended conservative tests: Grid candidates require a sign crossing from heightfield-minus-worldZ over all eight cell corners, and sphere candidates use the froxel face planes expanded by radius. This removes the all-cells hammer while avoiding representative-ray chopping.

A narrow horizontal visibility strip cannot be explained by curved Grid height misses alone. The failed corner-sign Grid test and froxel face-plane sphere test were still proxy tests for the wrong view-froxel shape. The current CPU bootstrap bins spheres with a conservative froxel bounding sphere and bins the Grid by solving actual height-sheet crossings per low-res screen tube, then marking crossed depth slice ranges.

The repeated horizontal strip was caused by CPU view-froxel binning using raw top-left pixel Y while the D3D12 scene shader flips input UV Y before ray generation. CPU candidate producers must mirror the shader screen-ray pixel convention exactly. Spheres now use projected screen bounds plus travel slice ranges instead of brute-force per-froxel testing, reducing bootstrap CPU cost.

Depth-sparse CPU binning for the Grid is the wrong bootstrap contract. At grazing angles the Grid is a global transparent height sheet and guessed crossing slice ranges produce sliced bands. Bin the Grid by low-res screen tube and write it to all depth slices for tubes that may see the Grid radius; the shader exact height-sheet intersection remains the authority for whether an event exists inside each interval.

The D3D12 transparent froxel candidate experiment made the Grid angle-dependent, spiky, and brittle. The Grid should follow the proven D3D11 contract: trace it directly as a stochastic surface before the nearest solid, write Grid identity/travel/normal/coverage for TAA, and do not leave dead transparent-surface binning code in the renderer. Git is the archive.

The D3D11 reference Grid used periodic line domains with fwidth() and pixel-width constants for stable line width. The D3D12 world-space ddx/ddy footprint made width angle-dependent. D3D12 now uses the D3D11 line/contour/field-line width math and a screen-pixel-stable hash dither; the remaining quality gap is binding Aetheria blue-noise texture for proper stochastic transparency.

D3D12 Grid stochastic coverage now uses Aetheria's copied 512x512 R8 blue-noise texture instead of a hash. The texture is uploaded once to a default-heap R8_UNorm resource, exposed through a transient SRV at t15, and sampled point/wrap at s1 in screen space without a frame-wide animated seed so per-pixel coverage stays stable for TAA.

Stable blue-noise dithering is too static for TAA to converge stochastic Grid coverage. The failure mode to avoid is global threshold flicker where all pixels toggle together. Use a per-frame low-discrepancy pixel offset into the blue-noise tile so each pixel changes threshold over time while the spatial mask remains decorrelated.

Stable temporal control, history age/weight, and field identity do not prove Grid TAA is smoothing color. D3D12 was still feeding stochastic hit/miss radiance into resolve/history. The fix is the D3D11 contract: post resolve reconstructs analytic Grid color from current Grid travel/world position using the Grid height target, nearest-loads Grid history, and skips color-delta rejection for Grid pixels while raw debug keeps showing the stochastic stream.

Once D3D12 Grid resolve reconstructs the expected stochastic value, line brightness must be tuned as averaged transparent radiance, not as sparse on-sample brightness. The earlier 0.24 alpha scale made Cartesian and isoline contributions collapse when the color path became mathematically honest. Keep scene stochastic selection and post analytic reconstruction using identical Grid color/alpha constants.

Do not present D3D12 loading frames before the initial shader pipelines are ready. Presenting a black/loading swapchain frame takes visible ownership away from the Win32 splash, and DirectWrite loading text can persist into later frames because it wrote into a live backbuffer. While the async pipeline task runs, the renderer should avoid touching the swapchain; the host can keep repainting the GDI splash until HasPresentedReadyFrame becomes true.

Current D3D12 startup splash behavior works but flickers slightly while the host repaints the Win32/GDI splash during async shader pipeline compilation. Treat that as a polish debt, not a reason to reintroduce swapchain loading frames. Future fix should either repaint only on invalidation/throttled intervals or use a proper pre-renderer splash state/window before D3D12 begins presenting.

A transparent Grid overlay must not become the visible scene packet. D3D12 was writing FIELD_ID_GRID, Grid travel, normal, coverage, and medium-to-Grid opacity even when fog/solid content behind it was the real scene. That made temporal debug views and volumetric history classify or clip fog by the Grid. The Grid is now a non-terminating stochastic color overlay added after solid/medium composition, while field identity/travel/control stay owned by the actual opaque or medium result. Future Grid stabilization needs a separate transparent-overlay temporal lane, not reuse of the scene packet.

Surface identity and medium identity cannot share one temporal metadata packet. A medium in front of a planet should have its own opacity, centroid travel, density, history age, and history weight even though the planet remains the hard ray terminator. D3D12 now emits a separate medium packet MRT and ping-pongs medium history beside surface history. Debug field identity can show medium where opacity exists; Grid/particles should follow with a separate stochastic-event packet rather than stealing surface or medium identity.

Stochastic transparent surfaces need their own temporal lane. The Grid now emits event color plus event metadata, and resolve accumulates that event history before compositing it over the surface+medium scene. Particles and billboard surfaces should join this sampled-event path rather than alpha blending or stealing surface identity.

A stochastic event temporal lane must retain history much more aggressively than opaque surface TAA. With a 0.92 event history cap, dither misses visibly erode low-coverage Grid lines and leave grain. Use very high validated retention for event radiance and label composite event/medium/surface debug views as lane identity rather than field identity.

Do not blue-noise dither a single transparent event candidate. Debug coverage/weight can look smooth while final breaks down if the event color lane still carries binary hit/miss radiance. For the current Grid-only event, write expected premultiplied radiance (`color * coverage`) and reserve blue-noise stochastic selection for a future multi-event chooser where several transparent samples compete.

Registered volumetric brushes must be anchored in world space, not Grid visibility space. D3D12 cloud ellipsoids were centered from `frame.Grid.Center`, so panning the camera/Grid dragged the medium with it. Store cloud centers as world-space simulation anchors and let the Grid move independently.

Interactive SDF bodies need their own object-motion reprojection data. The cursor SuperFormula body carries current and previous cursor world anchors in frame constants, and resolve maps current cursor hits to the previous cursor center before sampling history. Heavy specialized SDF traces should be invoked once per matching froxel, not from the generic packed-id helper for every int4 component, or shader compile time can explode.

For the cursor body, a revolved 2D profile is a better machine than a SuperFormula when the desired shape is an elongated spinning top. The implemented profile is evaluated top-to-bottom: the upper two-thirds grows from tip to waist with `3x^2 - 2x^3`, and the lower third shrinks from waist to bottom tip with `1 - sqrt(x)`. Keep this evaluator analytic and cheap; sampled profile-segment distance made D3D12 shader pipeline compile stall.

A revolved profile equation is not automatically a valid SDF. `radial - radius(z)` overestimates distance on sloped/grazing shoulders, which made the cursor top tear open from above. Scale the radial distance by `rsqrt(1 + slope^2)` and use smaller march advances so the field is conservative enough for sphere tracing.

Do not join a raw `1 - sqrt(x)` profile at `x = 0`; the infinite slope at the waist seam creates noisy normals and march instability. Soften the sqrt input with a small epsilon and renormalize the curve so the endpoints stay pinned while the seam slope becomes finite.

The cursor spinning-top profile reads better when the upper falloff is less abrupt and the waist shelf is smaller. Use shaped smoothstep with exponent 2.25 instead of 2.8, reduce waist radius from 0.78 to 0.70, and blend upper/lower profile radius and slope across a narrow waist band so it feels like a plausible object rather than two perfect curves meeting at a knife seam.

The cursor top needed more height and less high mass. Doubling the upper profile height to 2.50 local units and using a polynomial-shaped smootherstep (`x = t*t*(0.55 + 0.45*t)`, then quintic smootherstep) keeps the spire slim up high and flatter near the waist without reintroducing fractional `pow` compile stalls. The taller shape requires matching shader and CPU froxel bound radii of 1.46.

The symmetric spinning-top cursor read as a space station, and the first droplet/needle pass read as a candle flame. The live cursor profile is now a smaller droplet/needle locator: local z=-1 contacts the Grid, a modest belly peaks near z=-0.42, the needle ends near z=0.95, and CPU/HLSL bounds are 0.72. Keep this profile cheap; numeric slope probes and fractional pow made D3D12 initial pipeline compile exceed the headless startup timeout.

The segmented droplet/needle profile still read as a constructed seed/locator rather than a mathematical teardrop. The live cursor profile is a single asymmetric lobe with radius proportional to normalized `u * (1 - u)^2`, contact at local z=-1, tip at local z=1.15, and bound radius 0.72. This biases the mass downward while keeping the evaluator polynomial and cheap.

Cursor surface juice should compose through the SDF, not only through shading. The teardrop radius gets small signed latitudinal offsets from `sin(localZ * 28 - time * 4)`, so crests and troughs both exist in the actual SDF instead of only seeing the positive half-wave as outward shelves.

Cursor ripples should be SDF geometry, not emissive glow. The cursor material is now brass/metallic with Self-lit diffuse, brass specular, and Fresnel reflection; the old cyan glow was removed. The remaining constant ambient diffuse floors on cursor and planet shading were also cut so only diegetic light shapes solid bodies.

To avoid pagoda shelves and make the cursor bulb read more spherical, derive the lower profile from a sphere around local z=-0.46, blend into a strengthened cubic taper starting near local z=-0.10, and reduce ripple amplitude. Brass should read polished: use a tight high-power specular core plus a smaller shoulder, with no ambient diffuse floor.

Use the actual MathWorld teardrop curve for the cursor profile instead of hand-sculpted bulb/taper pieces. With x=cos(t) and y=sin(t)*sin^m(t/2), revolving around the x axis gives radius `sqrt(1 - x^2) * ((1 - x) / 2)^(m/2)`. The current shader uses m=6, avoiding fractional pow while giving the higher-exponent teardrop silhouette.

Cursor brass now uses a compact GGX microfacet metal model instead of Blinn-style tinted specular. It has brass F0, Schlick Fresnel, GGX distribution, Smith-Schlick visibility, no diffuse metal term, no glow, and no ambient floor. Until a reflection source exists, Self is the only direct radiance term.

Below-Grid pea-soup fog now enters the D3D12 medium path as real density/scattering. The medium pass and post ray-density debug bind the Grid height target at t22, use the actual height surface as the fog ceiling, and apply cheap Aetheria-style triangular-noise domain warping so final/debug views share the same procedural medium term.

D3D12 fog density no longer contains baked scattering color, and solid shading no longer uses a point-light radiance constant. Self is treated as a finite emissive sphere: medium in-scattering uses solid angle, Henyey-Greenstein phase, and cheap light-path transmittance; surfaces use emissive-sphere irradiance. Full noisy density inside the light-path propagation loop stalled shader pipeline compilation, so propagation uses a cheap extinction approximation while visible samples keep textured density.

The direct medium/surface lighting path now uses field-emitter data instead of hardcoded Self helpers. `FieldFlags.Emitter` plus `colorIntensity.rgb` inject radiance into D3D12 froxel medium samples and drive surface diffuse/specular lighting from the same table. Cursor is present as field 5 with zero emission until enabled; nebula/cloud fields can become emissive through data instead of bespoke shader branches.

D3D12 medium color history caused strong fog ghosting after emissive injection: medium-classified pixels used the main surface-history lane and the medium lane also blended previous full-frame color. Final resolve now excludes medium pixels from surface history and keeps mediumHistoryWeight at zero. Medium packet age/travel/density may remain diagnostic, but fog color history needs a real light-volume/medium reprojection contract before it can affect final presentation.

D3D12 medium radiance now has a separate temporal lane instead of borrowing final scene history. Scene writes `sceneMediumColor`; resolve subtracts current medium from scene color before surface history, reprojects medium packet travel to sample `historyMediumColor`, validates by medium travel/opacity/density, and composites resolved surface plus resolved medium plus events. Cold shader compilation may exceed the default 30s headless watchdog, but warm verification passes.

D3D12 medium lighting now has separate injection and propagated-light froxel targets. The medium pass writes density, transport, and raw emitter injection from `FieldFlags.Emitter`; a propagation pass samples neighboring froxels with density attenuation; scene fog integration and solid diffuse shading consume the propagated light field. Glossy cursor specular still needs a future directional/probe source rather than pretending RGBA froxel irradiance is enough for metal reflections.

Renderer-owned lighting needs its own inspection surface. D3D12 debug mode 12 now shows the propagated froxel light target directly, separate from froxel density, so lighting failures can be diagnosed as injection, propagation, or integration problems instead of guessed from the final composite.

Do not keep dead lighting math in the live medium pass. D3D12 final integration reads density/transmittance plus the propagated froxel light target; the old phase-scattered direct radiance path into `transport.rgb` was unused and has been cut. Docs/state must describe the current direct-traced Grid event lane, not the removed transparent-froxel Grid experiment.

Aquarium medium math should justify where light comes from before beauty tuning. D3D12 froxels now carry explicit density, extinction sigma_t, scattering sigma_s, albedo, slice transmittance, and raw emitter irradiance. Scene integration uses Beer-Lambert transmittance and isotropic single scattering sigma_s * irradiance / 4pi; direct ray transmittance debug uses the same coefficient evaluator. mediumTerms now means x extinction-per-density, y scattering albedo, z reserved phase/aniso, w density scale.

Medium lighting needs explicit directional/probe data once phase and glossy surfaces matter. Aquarium now carries a luminance-weighted dominant-light direction moment beside propagated froxel irradiance, uses it for Henyey-Greenstein scattering, and shades solids from the same field instead of private direct emitter loops.

A single froxel light direction is a lossy artistic approximation. Intersecting beams average, and opposed beams can cancel to low confidence and isotropic fallback. Keep the smear for now because it may look good, but future full transport needs multi-lobe, probe, or reservoir direction storage.

The propagated medium light field now uses explicit scratch/final froxel targets and four ping-ponged propagation iterations for irradiance plus dominant direction, replacing the single one-pass neighbor spread. This remains a compact diffusion approximation, but propagation state and cost are explicit.

Aetheria-style raymarch jitter only fights march-step aliasing because it marches per pixel. Aquarium froxel injection also has an 8x8 screen-space lattice, so medium samples must jitter both sub-froxel XY representative ray location and in-slice Z travel. D3D12 now uploads the Aetheria R8 blue-noise texture as a static structured buffer and uses animated blue-noise offsets for both axes before TAA accumulation.

Uniform jitter during medium injection is not enough if consumers quantize travel back to a single froxel slice. Surface shading must interpolate propagated froxel irradiance and dominant direction between slice centers, or moving solids pop between lighting slices.
